title: Immigration/Naturalization Legal Services * Human Trafficking Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Immigration/Naturalization Legal Services * Human Trafficking Issues 3 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Domestic Violence Issues Human Trafficking Issues Older Adults Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION SERVICES AGAINST EXPLOITATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Services Against Exploitation (SAE) serves victims and survivors of all forms of human trafficking with an emphasis on foreign-born, immigrant, and refugee victims and survivors. A team of trauma-informed advocates and staff provides culturally and linguistically responsive case management, immigration legal assistance, and community referrals. They meet survivors where they are and offer survivor-centered, wrap-around services throughout each survivor’s journey toward self-empowerment and liberation. 10301 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Survivors and victims of all forms of human trafficking. Culturally and linguistically specific services available for foreign-born, immigrant, and refugee survivors.
